description: Curated namespace-level admission and contract-readiness declarations. States do not promote capability or document lifecycle.

state_definitions.outline: Ownership and a broad boundary exist; detailed contract work is not admitted.
state_definitions.research: A governed research question or evidence set exists; implementation scope is not admitted.
state_definitions.decision-blocked: Material founder, professional, provider, regulatory, or architecture decisions prevent contract commitment.
state_definitions.contract-planned: At least one first-slice capability is admitted, but the family contract set is incomplete.
state_definitions.workstream-active: An admitted controlled-prototype workstream is implementing part of the family; completion is not implied.
state_definitions.prototype-evidenced: At least one admitted capability has verified controlled-prototype evidence; the family is not thereby complete.
state_definitions.deferred: The family is explicitly outside current delivery scope until its named trigger is met.

families.admission_trigger: A bounded AI workstream names deterministic fallback, tool authority, evaluation, privacy, cost, and first-slice non-essentiality.
blockers: No broad autonomous-AI scope
Controlled evaluation and provider evidence are not complete

families.admission_trigger: An Assets workstream resolves authoritative inventory versus discovered projections and declares lifecycle, custody, privacy, and service seams.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Infrastructure-discovery research remains CIR-BACK-023

families.admission_trigger: Complete WS2 evidence registration and independent workstream review before any claim beyond the admitted Product subset.
blockers: Required WS2 capability-dimension cells remain planned
Product and Inventory web/accessibility evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: Prototype 3 admits the exact POS, register, cash, return, receipt, stored-value, and offline capability subset after WS2 closure.
blockers: Prototype 3 workstream has not started
Provider, fiscal, offline, UI, accessibility, and recovery evidence remain open

families.admission_trigger: Founder decisions FDR-002 and FDR-003 establish the operating entity and billing/settlement currency before contract commitment.
blockers: FDR-002 is open
FDR-003 is open
No commercial-runtime workstream is admitted

families.admission_trigger: Admit only the first-slice Party projection and consent subset, with CRM-owned role data and no duplicate Party authority.
blockers: No CRM implementation workstream
Only a prototype first-slice subset is declared

families.admission_trigger: Admit webhook and event-delivery increments only with internal-event ownership, signing, replay, quota, privacy, and operational evidence.
blockers: First-slice webhook delivery is not complete
External publication and support policy remain Draft

families.admission_trigger: Each shared engine is admitted independently by a named workstream with an owner, consumer contracts, failure behavior, and first-slice depth.
blockers: The namespace aggregates engines at different depths
Several engine families have no admitted implementation workstream

families.admission_trigger: Limit the first slice to the accountant-handoff seams until qualified accounting evidence and a future Finance workstream admit deeper contracts.
blockers: Full General Ledger and financial statements are deferred
Qualified Guyana accounting review is absent

families.admission_trigger: Admit production statutory contracts only after qualified jurisdiction requirements, provider/certification evidence, contingency rules, and founder scope authority exist.
blockers: Production submission is explicitly deferred
Qualified Guyana fiscal evidence and certification are absent

families.admission_trigger: A Fleet workstream resolves asset ownership, driver eligibility, telematics/provider boundaries, maintenance, privacy, and jurisdiction rules.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Direct vehicle/provider and jurisdiction evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A Governance workstream defines obligation sources, control/evidence authority, assessment lifecycle, legal hold, segregation, and audit interoperability.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Regulatory and professional evidence is jurisdiction-dependent

families.admission_trigger: Complete WS2 durable delivery, capability evidence, UI/accessibility evidence, and independent review before claiming the admitted subset complete.
blockers: Required WS2 capability-dimension cells remain planned
Product and Inventory web/accessibility evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A Knowledge workstream defines controlled-document authority, publication/review lifecycle, retention/legal hold, external sharing, search, and AI boundaries.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Product documentation does not establish business-record knowledge contracts

families.admission_trigger: A Logistics workstream selects a bounded shipment/dispatch scope and resolves carrier, customs, trade-document, route, proof, and provider evidence.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Carrier, customs, device, and jurisdiction evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A future roadmap decision admits non-cash loyalty after stored value is proven and preserves Commerce ownership of monetary balances.
blockers: Advanced loyalty is explicitly deferred from the first slice
No admitted loyalty workstream

families.admission_trigger: An Assets/Maintenance workstream resolves asset, inventory, workforce, meter, warranty, failure, work-order, and correction authority.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Device, condition, predictive, and compliance evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A Manufacturing workstream chooses a bounded production model and resolves formulas/BOMs, genealogy, planning, consumption, output, scrap, rework, quality, and costing seams.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Planning, shop-floor device, costing, and direct product evidence remain limited

families.admission_trigger: A Marketing workstream defines consent source, audience projection, content, experiments, delivery providers, attribution limits, privacy, and deletion behavior.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Consent, messaging-provider, privacy, and attribution evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A future phase admits bounded extension discovery/installation; paid billing and payout additionally require FDR-008 and legal/provider gates.
blockers: Arbitrary core-process extension execution is prohibited
Paid marketplace and publisher payout are disabled

families.admission_trigger: Deepen beyond the evidenced Party-record subset only after ADR-0007 review and a workstream names matching, merge, privacy, and cross-domain projection evidence.
blockers: ADR-0007 remains Proposed
Matching, merge, restricted identifiers, and privacy depth remain incomplete

families.admission_trigger: Admit provider-specific prototypes only after FDR-001, FDR-006, and FDR-007 plus provider availability/certification evidence; preserve direct tenant-provider contracts.
blockers: Provider and terminal strategy decisions are open
Guyana availability, certification, settlement, dispute, and offline evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A jurisdiction-specific Payroll workstream is admitted only after qualified legal/tax evidence defines calculation, finalization, filing, payment, correction, privacy, and records obligations.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Qualified Guyana payroll, tax, filing, and provider evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A Planning workstream defines governed metric inputs, scenario/version authority, approvals, forecast lineage, write-back boundaries, and predictive-model evidence.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Governed metrics and operational write-back contracts are not selected

families.admission_trigger: Admit each remaining Platform capability through its named first-slice or later workstream; WS1 evidence applies only to the registered subset.
blockers: Only nine Platform capabilities have evidenced first-slice records
Offline, devices, numbering, import/export, privacy, search, jobs, files, and operations remain at mixed depths

families.admission_trigger: A Procurement workstream selects a bounded requisition-to-receipt scope and defines Party/supplier roles, approvals, matching, returns, Inventory and Finance seams.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Supplier, warehouse, invoice-matching, UI, and offline evidence is not implementation-ready

families.admission_trigger: A Projects workstream chooses a bounded delivery model and resolves configuration depth, assignment, time/expense, billing inputs, acceptance, correction, and reporting authority.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Configuration, billing, workforce, and customer evidence remains research-only

families.admission_trigger: A Rental workstream resolves asset/inventory authority, availability, reservation, deposits, payment, delivery/collection, inspection, damage, overdue, tax, and agreement rules.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Deposit/legal terms, devices, payments, and direct workflow evidence are absent

families.admission_trigger: Treat WS1 tenant-isolation evidence as one bounded result; deeper privacy/risk/protective-action work requires its own implementation and independent security evidence.
blockers: Only tenant isolation has a first-slice evidence record
Penetration, privacy exercise, risk-model, abuse, and operational evidence remains open

families.admission_trigger: A Service workstream chooses service-desk or field-service depth and resolves Party, assets, inventory, scheduling, SLA, entitlement, mobile, remote-action, and escalation authority.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
ITSM/MSP/RMM comparison remains CIR-BACK-021
Direct field/mobile evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A Warehouse workstream selects receiving/putaway/picking/count depth and resolves task authority, Inventory seams, devices, labels, offline behavior, labor privacy, and recovery.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Direct warehouse-device, offline, accessibility, and capacity evidence is absent

families.admission_trigger: A Workforce workstream selects a bounded worker/assignment scope and resolves Party linkage, effective dating, sensitive data, jurisdiction rules, self-service, offline, and Payroll seams.
blockers: No admitted roadmap workstream
Qualified employment/privacy/jurisdiction evidence is absent
Direct authenticated workflow evidence is limited
